model.model_alias_note: gpt-5-mini is an OpenAI alias that resolves to a dated snapshot at request time. The actual snapshot ID returned by the API is persisted in each diagnosis row's metadata.model_info.resolved_model, so a future re-run can verify whether the snapshot has changed underneath the alias.
model.temperature_note: gpt-5-mini is a reasoning-class model: temperature/top_p are ignored by the API. The shim does not send them.
model.max_completion_tokens_note: OpenAI's reasoning models use `max_completion_tokens` (capped here at 4096) instead of `max_tokens`. Reasoning tokens are not counted against this cap by the API.
model.json_mode_note: JSON-only output is requested via the system prompt + user message ('STRICT JSON ... No prose, no code fences, JSON only'). The shim's `parse_diagnosis_json` strips fence markers if any leak through.

context_policy.max_chars_note: Same cap as the Claude shim for cross-debugger comparability — contexts >480000 chars cause the shim to exit 1 with `unsupported_context_too_large` (post-Codex-2026-05-11 F1 fix; pre-fix the shim exited 0 with a placeholder body).

_doc: Added 2026-05-11 per Codex adversarial review on commit 772520d. The §3i cross-family validation run committed before this file existed used the same effective settings — see model card and §3i for the run-time invariants. This config is what FUTURE re-runs must match for the §3i numbers to remain reproducible. The shim records actual model_info into each row so any divergence between this config and a future run is detectable.
